title: fuertesimalva stipulata
intro: Fuertesimalva stipulata, also known as "yerba de la montaña" or "mountain herb," is a medicinal plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Elaeocarpaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its potential therapeutic uses. The health benefits associated with Fuertesimalva stipulata include the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to be consumed as a tea or used topically in the form of a poultice or ointment.
